text: Following a laparoscopic right colectomy for cecal cancer, the surgeon performs an isoperistaltic anastomosis. Two enterotomies are created to facilitate the insertion of a linear stapler.
Following a laparoscopic right colectomy for colon cancer, the surgeon performs an isoperistaltic anastomosis. Two enterotomies are created to facilitate the insertion of a linear stapler.
The surgeon places a suspension stitch to stabilize the tissue before inserting the linear stapler, ensuring proper alignment for firing.
The surgeon places a suspension stitch to facilitate the insertion of the linear stapler before firing.
The surgeon uses the suspension stitch to close the entrocolotomy, reinforcing a potentially vulnerable suture point with two layers of running absorbable sutures.

The surgeon completes the running suture, bringing it back to the initial starting point, ensuring closure of the surgical site.
